description:
You are helping a data-science team tidy up their working directory.

    Initial situation
    -----------------
    1. A directory called /home/user/datasets/raw/ already exists.  
       It contains the five files listed below:
         • customers.csv  
         • inventory.csv  
         • notes.json  
         • products.csv  
         • sales.csv  

    2. A sibling directory, /home/user/datasets/clean/, is present but **empty**.

    What you must do
    ----------------
    • Move **only** the four “*.csv” files from /home/user/datasets/raw/ into /home/user/datasets/clean/.  
      notes.json must stay where it is.

    • Immediately after the move, create a log file called /home/user/datasets/cleaning.log that lists the
      contents of /home/user/datasets/clean/ (i.e. the four CSV files you just moved).

    Required log-file format
    ------------------------
    • One filename per line.  
    • Alphabetical (lexicographic, ascending) order.  
    • The file must end with exactly one trailing newline character.  
    • No extra spaces, headers, or blank lines.

      Correct log contents **exactly**:

        customers.csv
        inventory.csv
        products.csv
        sales.csv

      (Each line terminated with “\n”, including the last.)

    Constraints
    -----------
    • Accomplish everything with **one single compound command** typed at the shell prompt
      (using pipes, “&&”, “;”, etc. is allowed, but it has to be one physical command line).

    Success criteria checked by the grader
    --------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/datasets/clean/ contains exactly the four CSV files and nothing else.  
    2. /home/user/datasets/raw/ contains notes.json and no “*.csv” files.  
    3. /home/user/datasets/cleaning.log exists and its contents are byte-for-byte identical to the
       specification above.
truth:
Initial filesystem snapshot
    ---------------------------
    Directory tree before the agent starts:

    /home/user/datasets/
        raw/
            customers.csv        (non-empty, arbitrary CSV content)
            inventory.csv        (non-empty, arbitrary CSV content)
            notes.json           (non-empty, arbitrary JSON content)
            products.csv         (non-empty, arbitrary CSV content)
            sales.csv            (non-empty, arbitrary CSV content)
        clean/                   (directory exists, is empty)

    Expected final state
    --------------------
    After successful task completion:

    1. /home/user/datasets/clean/ contains:
         • customers.csv
         • inventory.csv
         • products.csv
         • sales.csv

       The file contents are unchanged from their originals in “…/raw/”.

    2. /home/user/datasets/raw/ now contains only:
         • notes.json

    3. /home/user/datasets/cleaning.log exists with the following 58-byte payload
       (note: “\n” denotes a single Unix newline, ASCII 0x0A):

        customers.csv\n
        inventory.csv\n
        products.csv\n
        sales.csv\n

       Exact byte sequence:
       63 75 73 74 6f 6d 65 72 73 2e 63 73 76 0a
       69 6e 76 65 6e 74 6f 72 79 2e 63 73 76 0a
       70 72 6f 64 75 63 74 73 2e 63 73 76 0a
       73 61 6c 65 73 2e 63 73 76 0a

    The grader will confirm:
    • Exactly four CSV files are present in “…/clean/”.
    • “…/raw/” contains zero CSV files.
    • /home/user/datasets/cleaning.log matches the byte pattern above.
name: task_000000_07a7efcd
